description: Security of secrets: stop storing API keys in plaintext QSettings; prefer environment variables, OS-level credential stores (keyring), or make 'remember API key' explicit. Ensure keys are masked in logs and dialogues.
steps: Add 'keyring' to pyproject.toml optional dev/gui dependencies (it's a lightweight cross-platform credential store)

Create src/stx/gui/secrets.py module that: (1) tries to import keyring, (2) provides store_api_key(backend, key), retrieve_api_key(backend) -> Optional[str], delete_api_key(backend) functions, (3) falls back to env var lookup if keyring unavailable

In src/stx/gui/settings_dialog.py: (1) add a 'Remember API key' checkbox next to the API key field, (2) when checked + OK clicked, store via secrets.store_api_key(), (3) on load, retrieve from secrets.retrieve_api_key() if checkbox was previously checked, (4) remove storing the raw key in QSettings (translation/api_key)
In settings_dialog.py _load_values(), load the API key from the secrets module rather than from QSettings plaintext. Keep the QSettings key only as a boolean 'translation/remember_api_key' flag.
In src/stx/gui/workers.py TranslationWorker, ensure the api_key passed to the backend constructor is not logged. Already uses logging at DEBUG level - verify no key leakage.
In src/stx/translate/deepl_paid.py, azure.py, openai_llm.py: ensure LOGGER calls never include the api_key value (they currently don't log it, but add a guard comment)
Mask API keys in any GUI error dialogs: if an exception message contains something that looks like a key (long alphanumeric string), mask all but the first 4 chars
acceptance_criteria: API keys are NOT stored in QSettings INI file in plaintext
API keys are stored via keyring when available, or only in memory per-session
Log output never contains full API keys
If keyring is unavailable, falls back to env var with clear messaging
